commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9c05ea131156fd1b412527f71ca131bb1f70e239)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes Gemini `web_search` freshness shortcuts from Google Search grounding `timeRangeFilter` intervals to soft query recency instructions, updates cache partitioning, docs, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +42, Docs +2. Total +44 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction for the exact same-day Gemini rejection is present in this PR. Source inspection does confirm current main and v2026.6.9 send relative freshness as a hard `timeRangeFilter`, while the PR removes that hard filter for shortcuts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the Gemini freshness semantics plus contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: Fix Gemini web search freshness handling
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Gemini-backed `web_search` could fail when callers passed relative `freshness` values like `day`. OpenClaw mapped those shortcuts to Gemini `google_search.timeRangeFilter`, but the Gemini endpoint rejects some relative hard ranges, including the same-day shape seen in production, before any grounded result is returned.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps explicit `date_after` / `date_before` behavior as the hard Gemini Google Search grounding time range path, while changing relative `freshness` shortcuts to a query-level recency instruction. That preserves the user's intent to prioritize recent sources without sending an invalid hard filter to Gemini.

The cache key now includes the freshness shortcut even though the hard filter is omitted, so cached responses for the same query remain separated by recency intent.

## User Impact

Users can keep calling Gemini `web_search` with `freshness: "day"`, `week`, `month`, or `year` without the request being rejected by Gemini's time range validation. When a hard grounding range is required, users can still use `date_after` and `date_before`.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/google/web-search-provider.test.ts` passed.
- `pnpm test:extension google` passed: 26 test files, 349 tests.
- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/google/web-search-provider.test.ts` passed after fixing the PR-introduced `typescript(no-base-to-string)` lint finding from CI.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/google/src/gemini-web-search-provider.ts extensions/google/src/gemini-web-search-provider.runtime.ts extensions/google/web-search-provider.test.ts` passed.
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/tools/gemini-search.md docs/tools/web.md` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed with no introduced actionable correctness issues after the schema/docs update.
- Live operational validation before this PR: patched Gemini `web_search(freshness=day)` completed successfully on an OpenClaw gateway, with no Gemini `FIELD_INVALID` / `time_range_filter` errors in the post-deploy journal window.

Known gap: `pnpm build` and the current PR CI still fail outside this changed surface. The repeated base failures include `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.ts` importing missing `resolveEnforceFinalTagWithResolver`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-result-truncation.ts` missing `aggregateEligible`, and `src/plugins/contracts/speech-vitest-registry.ts` being reported as an unexpected unused file. This branch changes only the Gemini web search provider/docs/tests.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: yes. Prepared with Codex. I understand the changed code path: relative Gemini freshness no longer creates a hard `timeRangeFilter`; explicit date ranges still do; and cache partitioning keeps freshness-specific responses separate.
target.author: sunjae-k
